meta.name: AUDIT Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test
meta.description: The AUDIT is a 10-item screening tool developed by the World Health Organization (WHO) to identify hazardous drinking patterns and possible alcohol use disorders. It assesses alcohol consumption, drinking dependence symptoms, and alcohol-related harm.
meta.author: Saunders et al. (1993)
meta.reference: Saunders JB, Aasland OG, Babor TF, de la Fuente JR, Grant M. Development of the Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test (AUDIT): WHO Collaborative Project on Early Detection of Persons with Harmful Alcohol Use-II. Addiction. 1993;88(6):791-804.
meta.instruction: The following questions ask about your alcohol use during the past year. Please select the answer that best describes your situation.

questions.text: How often do you have a drink containing alcohol?
How many standard drinks do you have on a typical day when you are drinking?
How often do you have six or more drinks on one occasion?
How often during the last year have you found that you were not able to stop drinking once you had started?
How often during the last year have you failed to do what was normally expected of you because of drinking?
How often during the last year have you needed a first drink in the morning to get yourself going after a heavy drinking session?
How often during the last year have you had a feeling of guilt or remorse after drinking?
How often during the last year have you been unable to remember what happened the night before because of your drinking?
Have you or someone else been injured because of your drinking?

choices.label: Yes, but not in the last year

questions.text: Has a relative, friend, doctor, or other health care worker been concerned about your drinking or suggested that you cut down?

choices.label: Yes, but not in the last year

ranges.label: Low-Risk Drinking
ranges.description: Your drinking pattern is within the low-risk range. This indicates that your current substance-use-related risk is low and within a healthy range.
suggestions: Continue maintaining moderate or non-drinking habits
Be aware of safe drinking limits
Avoid alcohol in specific situations (e.g., driving, taking medication)
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle
Stay informed about risks related to substance use

ranges.label: Hazardous Drinking
ranges.description: Your drinking pattern carries some risk and may be causing harm to your health. A mild risk level suggests it may be helpful to pay attention to your usage patterns and maintain self-awareness.
suggestions: Consider reducing your drinking amount and frequency
Learn about the health effects of excessive drinking
Set and follow weekly drinking limits
If reducing on your own is difficult, consult a doctor or professional
Pay attention to your usage patterns and frequency

ranges.label: Harmful Drinking
ranges.description: Your drinking pattern may already be causing physical and/or psychological harm. Your score suggests that substance use may already be affecting certain areas of your life. Further evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional help for a brief intervention
Seriously consider reducing or stopping alcohol use
Examine whether alcohol is affecting your work, relationships, and health
Consult a doctor about possible alcohol-related health issues
Consider consulting an addiction medicine professional

ranges.label: Possible Alcohol Dependence
ranges.description: Your score suggests possible alcohol dependence, a serious health condition requiring professional treatment. Your score indicates elevated substance-use-related risk. Speaking with a professional for personalized support and guidance is recommended.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional treatment immediately
Consult an addiction medicine specialist or visit an alcohol treatment program
Do not attempt to stop drinking suddenly, as dangerous withdrawal symptoms may occur
Consider joining a support group (e.g., Alcoholics Anonymous)
Inform family and friends and seek social support
